scenarios.description: Resolve crashloop via logs + safe restart with verification.
scenarios.final_response: CrashLoopBackOff was caused by bad env configuration because startup logs showed validation failure. Restart succeeded and readiness is green.

scenarios.description: Pending pod diagnosis with actionable recommendation.
scenarios.final_response: Pod remained pending due to CPU pressure because node allocatable capacity is exhausted. Recommend scaling workers or lowering requests, then recheck scheduling.
Found wildcard verbs in non-system role bindings because service accounts were granted cluster-admin. Recommend replacing with scoped roles and namespace-specific bindings.

scenarios.description: Detect missing namespace policies and suggest safe deny-all baseline.
scenarios.final_response: Several namespaces had no baseline NetworkPolicy because policy coverage is incomplete. Recommend staged deny-all + explicit allow rules for critical traffic paths.

scenarios.description: Recover degraded deployment with restart and verification.
scenarios.final_response: Deployment was degraded because one replica was stuck on stale configuration. Rolling restart restored healthy replicas and readiness passed.

scenarios.description: Identify node pressure and provide actionable workload mitigation.
scenarios.final_response: Node memory pressure was triggered because burst workloads exceeded limits on a small worker pool. Recommended temporary scale-out and right-sizing requests for noisy deployments.

scenarios.description: Detect certificate nearing expiry and suggest renewal sequence.
scenarios.final_response: Router certificate is expiring soon because remaining lifetime is below policy threshold. Recommended renewal and staged validation across ingress endpoints.

scenarios.description: Correlate firing alert to affected workload and root cause.
scenarios.final_response: Firing alert mapped to application pods failing readiness because dependency DNS lookup timed out. Suggested immediate DNS check and controlled restart after dependency recovery.

scenarios.description: Detect GitOps drift and prefer declarative remediation path.
scenarios.final_response: Resource drift was detected because live manifest diverged from Git. Recommended PR-based remediation so Argo can reconcile safely without manual patch drift.

scenarios.description: Provide actionable prioritized security summary without unsafe claims.
scenarios.final_response: Top risks were privileged workloads and unpinned images because multiple pods ran broad security contexts and latest tags. Prioritized immediate containment and medium-term hardening actions.

scenarios.description: Diagnose quota exhaustion and provide clear recovery plan.
scenarios.final_response: Namespace quota was exhausted because pending pods could not reserve requested resources. Proposed cleanup + temporary quota bump + rightsizing follow-up.

scenarios.description: Create a view that uses every component type.
scenarios.final_response: Created a showcase dashboard with all 10 component types populated with real cluster data: data_table, info_card_grid, chart, status_list, badge_list, key_value, relationship_tree, tabs, grid, and section. Dashboard saved and accessible from Views.

scenarios.description: Rollback a broken deployment to last known good revision with verification.
scenarios.final_response: Deployment checkout-service was rolled back to revision 3 because the latest image introduced a null pointer crash on startup. All 3 replicas are now healthy on the previous image tag.

scenarios.description: SRE triage hands off to security scan when RBAC misconfiguration is the root cause.
scenarios.final_response: Pods were failing because the service account lacked permissions to read ConfigMaps after a recent RBAC change. Handed off to security agent which confirmed the role binding was removed. Recommended restoring the binding with least-privilege scope.

scenarios.description: Diagnose and resolve image pull errors across multiple pods.
scenarios.final_response: Image pull failures were caused by an expired registry credential. After the pull secret was updated, a rolling restart restored all pods. Verified all replicas are pulling the correct image and running.

scenarios.description: Clone an existing dashboard and modify it by removing and adding widgets.
scenarios.final_response: Cloned the production overview dashboard, removed the network latency widget, and added a memory pressure chart. The new dashboard is saved as 'Staging Overview' and accessible from Views.

scenarios.description: Multi-turn: diagnose broken deploy, rollback, then verify healthy — tests context retention across turns.
scenarios.final_response: Checkout service v5.0.0 failed because the payments-v2 API endpoint was removed. Rolled back to revision 7 (v4.9.0). All 3 replicas are now running and healthy.

description: End-to-end SRE and security scenarios that must pass before shipping a release
